math_recheck: Re-derived and numerically verified the full chain against the original 1928 German scan (eqs 31-46, 57-59) and OpenFermion: string v=prod(1-2N) (eq32, code mz=I-2N=diag(1,-1)=+Z standard), the slide rule b(1-2N)=-(1-2N)b (eq35), CARs {a,a}=0/{a^dag,a}=delta (eqs36/40), converse N^2=N and [N_r,N_s]=0 (eqs45-46), fluctuation N(1-N) (eqs57-59), matrix-element sign formula, XX->free-fermions telescoping with omega_k=-J cos k (Fourier-verified for N=6,8,10), and the Clifford coda (group order 2^{2K+1}=32, 17=2^{2K}+1 conjugacy classes, unique 2^K-dim irrep with 16*1+4^2=32) all confirmed; inclusive vs exclusive string shown identical since (1-2N)b=b.
fixes: reasoning.md:15 corrected wrong explicit Pauli combination 'b=sigma^- =(sigma^x - i sigma^y)/2' (that combo is the sigma^+ matrix, not b); restated as b=sigma^- lowering / b^dag=sigma^+ raising with occupied=spin-up convention made explicit and parity 1-2N=diag(1,-1)=-sigma^z given as an explicit matrix

answer.md:39-41 disambiguated the qubit/Pauli line: the OpenFermion-faithful Z=diag(1,-1)=1-2N (standard Pauli) is the parity = -sigma^z in the paper's sigma^z:=2N-1 convention, so the standard-Z string no longer silently clashes with the prose sigma^z
